message:
feat(apr): improve dashboard UX with help key alias and terminal size check

Two quality-of-life improvements to the interactive dashboard:

1. Help key alias (h/H):
   - Added 'h' and 'H' as alternatives to '?' for showing help
   - Many terminal users expect 'h' for help (like vim, less, man)
   - The '?' key requires shift on most keyboards, making 'h' more ergonomic
   - Both keys now trigger the same help overlay

   Before: case '?') echo "help" ;;
   After:  case '?'|h|H) echo "help" ;;

2. Terminal size validation:
   - Dashboard now checks terminal dimensions on each render cycle
   - Requires minimum 80 columns × 24 rows for proper display
   - If terminal is too small, shows clear error message with current size
   - User can resize terminal and press any key to retry
   - Prevents garbled output from line wrapping and truncation

   The check runs at the start of each render loop iteration, so resizing
   the terminal mid-session is handled gracefully. The dashboard will
   either render correctly after resize or continue showing the size
   warning until the terminal meets minimum requirements.

   Error message format:
   "Terminal too small for dashboard (${cols}x${rows})"
   "Resize to at least 80x24 and press any key to retry"

These changes follow the existing dashboard patterns: keyboard handling
in dashboard_handle_input() and rendering loop in show_dashboard().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
